topic.title: Cassandra
topic.text: Style: Narrative Nonfiction (The New Yorker / Investigative Atlantic). The Narrative Arc: The Cassandra Tragedy. Frame the data as a "prophecy of the inevitable" that the world is incentivized to ignore. Techniques: Second-person narrative ("You"), vivid scene-setting, dramatic data presentation, and strong, active verbs. Constraint: Do NOT invent people or quotes. Use the "You" narrative to place the reader in the seat of the analyst who sees the truth. Mathematical Integration: Use LaTeX for formal equations. Let the math serve as the "divine sight" that reveals the hidden corruption. Tone: Urgent, slightly haunting, provocative, and intellectually grounded. The "Cassandra" Hook: Focus on the Asymmetry of Bullshit—how much harder it is to prove a truth than it is to manufacture a lie.
